title: Traffic-release and rival-window counterfactual features
summary: F1 strategy sources repeatedly emphasize clear air, traffic gaps, and undercut/overcut windows.
rationale: F1 strategy sources repeatedly emphasize clear air, traffic gaps, and undercut/overcut windows. Existing best code has circuit priors and rough wait-vs-pit heuristics, but it still lacks a strong local field-state representation tied to nearby rivals and likely rejoin traffic.
implementation_hint: Within each `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` build local-neighbor features by sorting on `Position`: tyre-age, lap-delta, degradation, and position-change differences to cars at `pos-2..pos+2`; add field percentiles and a proxy `clear_air_after_pit` score from estimated pit-loss and nearby-position pace. Also try a simple counterfactual delta: pit-now versus stay-out-one-lap under local traffic assumptions.
expected_effect: Most likely to help in edge cases where the same tyre state should imply opposite actions depending on nearby traffic or undercut threat.
risk: Position is only a coarse proxy for actual time gap, so naive neighbor features can be noisy. They may need robust clipping or race-specific normalization.
